mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and investment demand, which depresses corporate revenue forecasts and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.02 over any 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate liquidity concerns and margin calls, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during periods of high market leverage.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat in over 60% of observations when INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 3.0% force the Federal Reserve to accelerate monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation shocks that weaken wage growth and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields a positive rank IC greater than 0.03 over a 4-year rolling window, contradicting the negative return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly expansions in real gross domestic product reflect broad economic strength that supports corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with GDPC1 growth rates across multiple 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in industrial output and retail sales during a credit crunch regime signals severe demand destruction that overwhelms monetary stimulus, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both decline MoM
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 65% of instances when both INDPRO and RSAFS decline simultaneously during identified credit stress periods.
